markers.title: 'Channel' column header is semantically wrong
markers.body: The column labeled 'Channel' actually shows room type + rate plan names. Channels (Booking.com, Expedia, direct) are nowhere visible. Either relabel the column to 'Room / Rate' or restructure so channels appear as the row grouping.

markers.title: Section-row numbers have no label — meaning is unclear
markers.body: The numbers in room-type header rows (e.g. 5, 5, 5, 5, 5 under 'Double Room') appear to be available unit counts, but there is no column header or label confirming this. A user could mistake them for prices. Add a 'Avail.' header above.

markers.title: SAT/SUN columns highlighted with no legend
markers.body: The SAT 27 and SUN 28 columns have a coral/salmon background — but no legend explains why. Is it weekend demand? A selected date? High occupancy? A key next to the grid would make this self-explanatory.

markers.title: All rates identical across every day — demo data issue
markers.body: Every rate plan shows the same value for all five days (e.g. 240 / 240 / 240 / 240 / 240). Distribution's core value proposition is dynamic pricing — flat identical rates make the screen read as a loading error and undercut the prototype's story. Seed at least one day with weekend or demand uplift.

markers.title: Date format inconsistency — comma vs no comma
markers.body: Distribution shows '26 June, 2026' (with comma) while Calendar and Reservations show '26 June 2026' (no comma). Standardise the date format across all screens.

working_well: Room type → rate plan hierarchy (section header + sub-rows) is a clean, scannable structure for a dense data grid.
'Bulk update' as a prominent CTA signals the primary action clearly.
Filter chips (Room types, Rate plans, Channels) are the right granularity for narrowing a large rate grid.
Today's column (FRI 26) is clearly highlighted with an orange underline.
worth_considering: Show OTA channel logos (Booking.com, Expedia) as row groupings so the screen delivers on the 'Distribution' label — right now it reads more like a rate sheet.
Add a 'closed / stopped' state to individual cells (e.g. strikethrough or lock icon) to demonstrate stop-sell functionality.
Consider a mini sparkline or colour gradient in the rate cells to communicate demand-based pricing at a glance without opening each cell.
